title.en: supermemoryai/supermemory: A Memory Engine and API for the AI Era
title.zh: supermemoryai/supermemory：面向 AI 时代的记忆引擎与 API

summary.en: Supermemory pitches itself as the Memory API for the AI era — a fast, scalable engine for storing, querying and retrieving long-term context across agents and applications. The repo crossed 23k stars and added +236 in a day, landing on GitHub TypeScript Trending. Functionally it sits in the same emerging slot as Mem0, Letta and the Anthropic-flavoured memory plugins: provide an external system-of-record so agents don't depend on context windows or per-session caches. The README emphasises speed and a unified API across user-level and agent-level memory. For teams building agentic products that need persistent recall (Codex-style profiles, OpenClaw-style assistants), it's worth a serious side-by-side against alternatives.
summary.zh: Supermemory 把自己定位成「AI 时代的 Memory API」——一个快且可扩展的引擎，专门做跨 Agent / 跨应用的长程上下文存取与检索。仓库星数已破 2.3 万，单日新增 236 颗，登上 GitHub TypeScript Trending。功能位上和 Mem0、Letta、各家 Anthropic 风味的记忆插件直接对位：给 Agent 一个外部的 system-of-record，不再依赖上下文窗口或会话内 cache。README 强调速度与「用户层记忆 + Agent 层记忆」统一接口。对正在做需要持久记忆的 agent 产品（Codex 风格个人页、OpenClaw 风格助手）的团队，值得和同位项目认真比一遍。

title.en: Sam Altman: OpenAI Robotics Is Hiring for a Full-Stack Hardware Push
title.zh: Sam Altman：OpenAI Robotics 全栈招聘，正式入局物理 AI

summary.en: Sam Altman publicly opened OpenAI Robotics hiring, looking for full-stack hardware, ops, systems and ML engineers to "program and manufacture robots useful for society." The tweet crossed 12k likes and is the first time OpenAI Robotics has been talked about in the open as a real first-class effort with manufacturing ambitions rather than an exploratory team. Strategically it lands the same day NVIDIA dropped Cosmos 3 and Vera CPU — both pitched at the same physical-AI stack — making physical AI the single most concentrated theme of the day across the industry. Worth watching as a signal of where the next round of frontier-lab capital is going.
summary.zh: Sam Altman 公开开放 OpenAI Robotics 的招聘——他要的是「能从硬件、运维、系统到 ML 全栈做下来」的人，目标是「编程和制造对社会有用的机器人」。这条推突破 1.2 万赞，也是 OpenAI Robotics 第一次以「具备制造能力的一线团队」形象、而非「探索性小组」的形象公开露面。在战略时点上很巧——同一天 NVIDIA 发布 Cosmos 3 和 Vera CPU，定位的都是同一条物理 AI 栈，使物理 AI 成为当日行业最集中的主题。值得作为「下一轮前沿大厂资本去向」的信号观察。

title.en: CS336: Stanford's "Language Modeling from Scratch" Course Goes Public
title.zh: 斯坦福 CS336「Language Modeling from Scratch」课程材料开放

summary.en: Stanford's CS336 — "Language Modeling from Scratch" — released its full course site, walking through implementing modern LLMs end-to-end: tokenization, transformer architecture, training infrastructure, post-training and evaluation. The course is taught by Tatsu Hashimoto and Percy Liang and is one of the rare graduate-level treatments that actually digs into how to read training-time bottlenecks rather than stopping at the math. The slides and homework assignments are public, which makes it one of the strongest "self-study LLM" curricula currently online. Hacker News ranked it at 222 points within hours of being posted.
summary.zh: 斯坦福 CS336「Language Modeling from Scratch」开放了完整课程网站，端到端覆盖现代 LLM 实现：分词、Transformer 架构、训练基础设施、后训练与评测。课程由 Tatsu Hashimoto 与 Percy Liang 讲授，是少数真正深入到「训练时性能瓶颈如何被读出来」层面的研究生课程，而不是停留在数学推导。讲义和作业全部开放，是目前最强的「自学 LLM」课程材料之一。Hacker News 上线几小时已上到 222 分。

title.en: Prompt Injection in ChatGPT for Google Sheets Can Exfiltrate Entire Workbooks
title.zh: ChatGPT for Google Sheets 可被 prompt 注入外泄整个工作簿

summary.en: PromptArmor disclosed a prompt-injection attack in OpenAI's ChatGPT integration for Google Sheets that lets a maliciously-crafted spreadsheet (sent or shared to the victim) read and exfiltrate other cells in the workbook back out through the model's chat surface. The report walks through the exact prompt chain, the indirect-prompt-injection variant it exploits, and the missing isolation that should have prevented cross-cell read. This is the most production-relevant prompt-injection demo this month: it lands on a Google Workspace user without anything that looks suspicious, and the attacker controls only the spreadsheet content. Workspace admins should re-check the ChatGPT add-on permissions.
summary.zh: PromptArmor 披露了 OpenAI 在 Google Sheets 上的 ChatGPT 集成中的一种 prompt 注入攻击：攻击者只需把一份精心构造的电子表格分享给受害者，模型就会被诱导读取该工作簿的其他单元格并通过聊天界面把内容回传给攻击者。报告里给出了完整的 prompt 链路、所利用的 Indirect Prompt Injection 变种，以及本应阻止跨单元格读取的隔离缺失之处。这是本月最贴近生产场景的 prompt 注入演示之一——它能命中 Google Workspace 用户而完全不出现可疑迹象，攻击者也只控制电子表格内容。Workspace 管理员应该重新核查 ChatGPT 插件的权限范围。

title.en: Malicious npm Packages Detected Across Red Hat Cloud Services
title.zh: Red Hat Cloud Services 被发现引入恶意 npm 包

summary.en: A GitHub issue under RedHatInsights/javascript-clients flagged a series of malicious npm packages that had slipped into the dependency closure of Red Hat's hosted JavaScript clients. The thread traces how the bad packages got there (typosquats plus a compromised maintainer chain) and which downstream Red Hat Cloud Services touched them before being scrubbed. As a supply-chain incident this is one more data point that npm-side typosquat + maintainer-compromise attacks are now hitting larger-vendor cloud SDKs, not just hobby projects. If your stack pulls Red Hat JS clients transitively, walk your lockfile audits this week.
summary.zh: RedHatInsights/javascript-clients 仓库下的一个 GitHub issue 披露：一系列恶意 npm 包通过依赖闭包进入了 Red Hat 自家托管 JavaScript 客户端的链路中。讨论里追溯了恶意包是怎么进来的（typosquat + 受污染的维护者链）、以及在被清理之前哪些下游 Red Hat Cloud Services 引用了它们。作为供应链事件，这又一次说明：npm 上的 typosquat + maintainer 入侵已经能命中大型厂商的云 SDK，而不只是业余项目。如果你的栈传递性地拉取 Red Hat JS 客户端，本周建议立即跑一遍 lockfile 审计。

title.en: OpenAI Expands Codex Beyond Engineering with Plugins, Sites and Annotations
title.zh: OpenAI 把 Codex 推向工程团队之外：插件、Sites 与 Annotations 全面开放

summary.en: OpenAI's latest Codex update is the clearest sign yet that the company no longer sees Codex as just a coding assistant — it's becoming OpenAI's horizontal in-product agent for analysts, marketers, designers and investors. The post introduces three concrete primitives: Codex plugins (third-party tool integrations that show up inside ChatGPT/Codex sessions), Codex sites (shareable agent surfaces for specific workflows) and annotations (structured comments agents can leave on documents and assets for human review). The pitch is that the same agent loop running in IDEs can now run in Notion, Figma and CRM-style surfaces. For teams designing internal AI tooling, this is the first time OpenAI has shipped a sanctioned non-IDE delivery surface for Codex — worth tracking even if you don't ship anything yet.
summary.zh: OpenAI 最新 Codex 更新明确传达一个信号：Codex 不再只是「写代码助手」，而是 OpenAI 在 ChatGPT 之内、面向分析师、市场、设计师、投资人等所有角色的横向 agent 入口。这次更新引入三个新原语：Codex plugins（在 ChatGPT/Codex 会话里直接接入第三方工具）、Codex sites（针对特定工作流的可分享 agent 界面）、annotations（agent 在文档与素材上留下供人审阅的结构化批注）。相同的 agent 循环现在可以跑在 Notion、Figma、CRM 风格界面之中。对设计企业内部 AI 工具的团队来说，这是 OpenAI 第一次官方提供 IDE 之外的 Codex 分发面，值得关注后续生态走势。

title.en: OpenAI Frontier Models and Codex Land on AWS
title.zh: OpenAI 前沿模型与 Codex 正式登陆 AWS

summary.en: OpenAI announced that its frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS, ending the cloud's awkward position as the one hyperscaler without first-party OpenAI access. Practically this means AWS customers can call OpenAI models — and run Codex agents — under their existing AWS billing and IAM, instead of routing traffic through Azure OpenAI or directly via OpenAI's API. The deal is also the cleanest signal yet that OpenAI's multi-cloud strategy is real: Microsoft is no longer the exclusive distribution partner. For platform teams that have been blocked on "we can't use Azure" procurement battles, this removes one of the last barriers to standardising on OpenAI inside an AWS estate.
summary.zh: OpenAI 宣布前沿模型与 Codex 正式登陆 AWS，结束了「唯一没有 OpenAI 一方接入的超大规模云」这一尴尬局面。实操层面，AWS 客户从此可以在现有账单与 IAM 体系下直接调用 OpenAI 模型并运行 Codex agent，不必再绕道 Azure OpenAI 或直连 OpenAI API。这次合作也是迄今最干脆的信号——OpenAI 的多云策略落地，Microsoft 不再是独家分发伙伴。对那些卡在「公司不让用 Azure」采购流程上的平台团队，最后一道在 AWS 体系内全面采用 OpenAI 的门槛被搬开了。

title.zh: Anthropic 扩大 Project Glasswing 项目规模

summary.en: Anthropic published an update expanding Project Glasswing, its internal effort focused on transparency and interpretability tooling around frontier models. The expansion broadens the program's scope and partner set, with more grants, more external research partners and a clearer commitment to publishing interpretability findings even when they are not commercially flattering. The move continues Anthropic's positioning as the lab most willing to underwrite interpretability work that doesn't directly ship in a product. For safety researchers, this is also one of the better-funded venues to do mechanistic interpretability on production-scale Claude variants without having to assemble compute on your own.
summary.zh: Anthropic 发文宣布扩大 Project Glasswing 项目——这是其围绕前沿模型透明度与可解释性工具的内部计划。这次扩展拓宽了资助范围和合作伙伴池，承接更多外部研究人员，并更明确地承诺即使结论对自身不利也会公开发表。Anthropic 持续强化「最愿意为不直接转化为产品的可解释性工作买单」的形象。对安全研究者而言，这也是少数能在产品级 Claude 变体上做机制可解释性研究、还不必自己凑算力的「钱够多」的渠道之一。

title.en: Codex Discovered a Hidden HTTP/2 Bomb
title.zh: Codex 发现一处隐藏的 HTTP/2 协议炸弹

summary.en: Calif.io published a writeup of a previously-undocumented HTTP/2 DoS vector that OpenAI's Codex surfaced while a user was poking at protocol code. The interesting part is not just the bug — a crafted frame sequence that lets a single peer balloon server-side memory disproportionate to bandwidth — but the discovery path: Codex flagged the pattern as suspicious during routine code review, the user verified it against the spec, and ended up with a real CVE-class finding. This is one of the cleaner real-world examples of an AI coding agent doing genuine vulnerability research rather than auto-completing test cases, and it makes a concrete case that 'have Codex grep for protocol footguns' belongs in a security team's review pipeline.
summary.zh: Calif.io 发文披露了一处此前没有正式记录的 HTTP/2 DoS 向量——这是用户在用 OpenAI Codex 翻协议代码时被 Codex 指出来的。看点不只是漏洞本身（一段构造好的帧序列可以让单个对端把服务器内存撑到与带宽完全不成比例），更是发现路径：Codex 在常规 code review 中把这段模式标记为可疑，用户对照 RFC 复核，最后落到一处 CVE 级别的真实缺陷。这是「AI 编码 agent 做真正意义上的漏洞研究、而不是补全测试用例」少见的清晰案例，也给出了一个具体论据：「让 Codex 全仓搜协议级 footgun」应该被安全团队的代码审查管线接入。

title.en: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
title.zh: VSCode 漏洞导致 GitHub Token 可被一键窃取

summary.en: Ammar Askar disclosed a 1-click exploit that uses a VSCode bug to lift a user's GitHub OAuth token simply by getting them to click a link. The chain abuses how VSCode handles a particular URL scheme, lets an attacker-controlled extension or webview obtain the same auth surface as the user's signed-in GitHub session, and ultimately exfiltrates the token without an additional prompt. Both Hacker News (607 points) and Lobsters picked it up as the security writeup of the day. For anyone running VSCode with a GitHub account signed in — which is essentially the whole ecosystem — the headline takeaway is to upgrade past the patched build immediately; the secondary one is that GitHub's auth surface inside editors has now had enough incidents to deserve a dedicated threat model.
summary.zh: Ammar Askar 披露了一个 1-click 利用：通过 VSCode 中的一处缺陷，只要诱导用户点击一个链接，就可以盗取该用户的 GitHub OAuth token。攻击链滥用 VSCode 处理特定 URL scheme 的方式，让攻击者控制的扩展或 webview 获得与用户已登录 GitHub 会话相同的鉴权能力，并在无二次确认的情况下把 token 外带出去。Hacker News（607 分）和 Lobsters 都把它选为当日安全要闻。对几乎所有「VSCode 里登录着 GitHub 账号」的用户——也就是整个生态——首要动作是立即升级到补丁版本；次要结论是：编辑器内的 GitHub 鉴权面已经攒够事故、值得有一份独立的威胁模型。

title.en: Stanford Study: AI Outperforms Law Professors at Office-Hour Questions
title.zh: 斯坦福研究：在「office hours」问题上，AI 已优于法学教授

summary.en: A Stanford Law study (Salinas et al.) had law professors write out the kind of questions they get asked in office hours, then collected answers from both Gemini 2.5 and human law professors, and finally had other law professors blind-judge the results. Gemini scored a 75% win rate against human professors, and — importantly — Gemini's answers were rated as LESS harmful than the humans'. The paper also notes that newer frontier models do even better. Both HN (381 points, 334 comments) and Ethan Mollick's tweet (801 likes) treated this as the headline 'GPT-4 passes the bar' moment for legal pedagogy: the bottleneck for replacing professor-style legal guidance with AI is no longer answer quality on standard student questions; it's the institutional pieces around accreditation, liability and trust.
summary.zh: 斯坦福法学院的一项研究（Salinas 等）让法学教授写出他们在 office hours 经常被问到的问题，由 Gemini 2.5 与真人法学教授分别回答，再请另一批法学教授盲评。结果是：Gemini 对真人教授的胜率达到 75%，并且——关键的一点——Gemini 的回答被评为「更不容易造成伤害」。论文还指出更新的前沿模型表现更好。HN（381 分、334 条讨论）和 Ethan Mollick 的推（801 likes）都把它当作法律教育领域的「GPT-4 过了律考」时刻：在标准学生问题上「用 AI 取代教授式法律指导」的瓶颈，不再是答案质量，而是认证、责任、信任这些制度层面的部件。
